scratch: Sprint 3 queue seeded 2026-07-08 from the deep repo audit (docs/audits/2026-07-08-repo-audit.md). NOT YET LAUNCHED. Read the SPRINT_3.md launch checklist first. LAUNCHER: working_dir and expected_gh_user in this file are MACHINE-LOCAL - after copying this staged queue to .claude/sprint-queue.json, set working_dir to YOUR absolute repo path and expected_gh_user to YOUR GitHub login (the account git pushes as). No expected_gcloud_config: this project has no GCP surface, loop-sprint.md preflight 0.6 self-skips. ENVIRONMENT: see ONBOARDING.md for zero-to-loop machine setup (Docker Desktop, Node LTS, gh, e2e deps). If port 3000 is taken on your box, set WEB_PORT in the root .env (gitignored) - e2e resolves it automatically via e2e/helpers/baseUrl.ts; canonical/CI stays :3000. If gh's active account silently flips (multi-account keyrings), run 'gh auth switch --user <your-login>' before gh writes. Prior sprint: sprint-2 11/11 done, merged as PR #19; lessons in CONTEXT.md.
gates: export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T api pytest -m unit -q
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T web sh -lc "cd /app && pnpm -F web exec tsc --noEmit"

cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && NODE_DIR=$(ls -d "$LOCALAPPDATA/Microsoft/WinGet/Packages/OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS"*/node-v*-win-x64 2>/dev/null | head -1) && export PATH="$PATH:$NODE_DIR" && npx -y prettier@3.9.4 --check "**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,md,yml,yaml}"
notes_global: e2e on the HOST via the winget node.exe + e2e/node_modules/@playwright/test/cli.js (plain npx playwright tries a global install); base URL resolved from root .env WEB_PORT by e2e/helpers/baseUrl.ts (this box :3001, canonical/CI :3000). Full suite ~17-23min; known flake = dev-server cold-compile timeout, isolated re-run clears it. Docker CLI needs the PATH export per shell. After ANY apps/web or packages/* source edit: docker compose up -d --force-recreate web BEFORE e2e (Windows bind-mount hot-reload does not fire). New python module under app/ needs docker compose restart api. After editing apps/web/package.json: reinstall INSIDE the web container. pytest: run detached, poll for PYTEST_EXIT=. NEW THIS SPRINT: the third gate (host prettier format:check, lockfile-pinned 3.9.4) is mandatory before every commit - CI enforces it and the sprint-2 loop shipped unformatted files. Seed idempotent: docker compose exec -T api python scripts/seed_demo.py. Logins admin@kentro.example / client@atlas.example, DemoPass!2026; spec-created users need unique timestamped emails. LLM is fixture-mode, offline, deterministic (D-017). Resolve seeded ids ONLY via e2e/helpers/ids.ts. Playwright gotchas: getByRole name matching is SUBSTRING; click()+waitForResponse not check() on auto-save; assert post-Run-AI state after page.reload(); no body click before first Tab in skip-link tests.

tasks.title: Fix CSF live-mode Run-AI: prompt/parser schema + payload grounding + contract test
verify: pytest -m unit green including new contract tests
contract test parses a prompt-schema-compliant response and asserts changes > 0
payload test asserts interview answers/evidence present in the job payload
full e2e green (s3/s5/s7 exercise fixture Run-AI)
tasks.notes: Audit critical find. (a) _CSF_SCORE_PROMPT (app/ai/jobs.py:41-54) says return {"subcategories":[...]} but the route parses data.get("scores",[]) keyed tier+subcategory_code (routes/csf.py:1099-1128) - live mode silently discards a compliant response. Align them (fixture shape is fine; update the prompt). (b) csf_score payload sends only tier/subcategory codes, no interview answers/evidence, though the prompt claims they're supplied - copy ZT's approach (routes/zt.py:398-400); payload goes through the redactor as designed. Keep D-017 fixture determinism. FAIL LOUDLY: consider logging a warning when a run-AI response parses to zero changes.

tasks.title: Port T7 draft-exists guard to attack + ZT (+ tech_debt if applicable) mint routes
verify: unit tests: exists->200 same id, not-exists->201 v1, closed-then-mint->201 v+1, for both routes
full e2e green - s5/s6/s11 mint via page.request.post and must handle the reuse response
tasks.notes: Pattern source: routes/csf.py:364-381 + tests/unit/test_csf_draft_guard.py (T7, sprint 2). attack mint (routes/attack.py:~243) pre-seeds ~600 coverage rows per version - the worst offender. zt mint at routes/zt.py:~471 (~87 rows). Check tech_debt for the same prior.version+1 pattern and guard it too if present. Follow s7's close-then-mint approach from T7 where specs need a genuinely fresh draft (fixture Run-AI is idempotent per assessment so reuse is deterministic).

tasks.title: Auth compensating controls: forced re-auth + refresh rotation, dead flags fail loudly, docs corrected
verify: unit: refresh past shield_forced_reauth_seconds -> typed 401 reason=reauth_required
unit: refresh rotation - reused old refresh token rejected
unit: startup with shield_auth_require_mfa=true raises a clear configuration error
full e2e green (specs sign in fresh; defaults must not trip them)
README/BUILD_REPORT describe only controls that exist
tasks.notes: README.md:137 + BUILD_REPORT A07 claim daily forced re-auth + 30-min idle timeout; neither is enforced (config.py:70-71 flags referenced nowhere; routes/auth.py:315-333 refresh re-issues indefinitely). Implement: original-auth-time claim honored at /auth/refresh (typed 401 reason=reauth_required past shield_forced_reauth_seconds, default 24h); rotation via jti denylist in Redis or rotating-pair check (coordinate with T3's Redis usage - shared helper welcome); dead MFA/email-verify flags (config.py:58-59) raise at startup if set true (flows don't exist - pretending is a fail-loudly violation); web handles reauth_required with friendly copy + redirect to sign-in. The 30-min refresh TTL IS effectively the idle timeout - document it as such rather than invent new machinery. D-016 typed-error envelope throughout. Append a DECISIONS entry for the chosen posture.

tasks.title: Rate limiting on auth + run-AI routes (Redis)
verify: unit: exceeding the window -> typed 429 reason=rate_limited with Retry-After
unit: Redis unavailable -> request allowed + loud structlog warning (fail-open, tested)
full e2e green (limits generous enough for the serialized suite)
tasks.notes: Redis is composed and idle (D-015 Part F placeholder note). New module app/security/rate_limit.py: fixed-window per-IP + per-account on /auth/login + /auth/register; per-client on the five run-AI endpoints. Config-driven limits with e2e-safe defaults (document numbers in the commit body). D-016 typed 429. Fail-open on Redis outage with a loud warning - an infra blip must not brick auth - and unit-test that path. NEW python module -> docker compose restart api. Tests run SQLite/no-Redis: the limiter must be dependency-injectable/fakeable.

tasks.title: Spec 15.5 filename convention for CSF Playbook + Risk Register exports
verify: unit: exported filenames carry Company_Name + Service_Name + MMDDYY (+ version suffix)
e2e s7 + s8 green with updated name assertions
tasks.notes: Master Spec 15.5 says EVERY download link complies. deliverable_filename() (apps/api/app/tech_debt/filename.py) already serves the four service deliverable routes. CSF Playbook 5-file export (routes/csf.py:~1202-1259, CSF_Playbook_v{n}*) and Risk Register (routes/risk.py:~332-356, Risk_Register_v{n}.*) bypass it. Route through the helper, preserve version suffixes. s7/s8 assert artifact names - update them (they delta-assert; verify exact matching mode first).

tasks.title: llm_calls tenant attribution (client_id, additive migration)
verify: unit: a run-AI call writes an llm_calls row with client_id set, on all five purposes
migration is batch_alter_table / SQLite-safe; old rows parse unchanged (C0)
tasks.notes: Audit 3b minor: llm_calls has only nullable service_id (models/llm_call.py:45) and risk-synthesis passes no service_id (routes/risk.py:206-216) - the biggest cross-assessment egress payload is the least attributable. Add nullable client_id, thread from the five call sites (tech_debt/extract.py:186, routes/attack.py:488, routes/csf.py:~1099, routes/risk.py:206, routes/zt.py:391); give risk at least the client. Additive only - C0 pattern.

tasks.title: Docs truth pass: architecture.md rewrite, README, DECISIONS dedupe, reviewer purge, ops docs
verify: grep -ri reviewer apps/api/app returns zero hits in docstrings/summaries (tests may keep historical mentions with a comment)
architecture.md describes multi-tenant/no-worker/audit_entries/no-unredact reality
no duplicate D-NNN headings in DECISIONS.md
pytest + tsc green (docstring edits touch python files)
tasks.notes: Every audit section-2 finding. architecture.md is the worst offender (single-tenant claim, Celery worker, audit_events, redactor.unredact). README: tenancy, phantom runbooks/terraform (say planned or remove), real test matrix, current e2e counts. CHANGELOG: dedupe the two [3.0.0] headings. DECISIONS: append-only - add an erratum entry renumbering the SECOND D-015 (Part F, line ~134) and an entry recording D-005/D-006 supersession (reviewer + release flow removed by Work Order A1/A3; note sprint-5 may reintroduce release-to-client deliberately). Purge '(admin/reviewer)' from OpenAPI summaries (routes/admin.py:144,197 - visible at :8000/docs) and stale docstrings (dependencies.py:9,97,115; security/jwt.py:12; schemas/auth.py:52; routes/intake.py:15). operations.md/development.md: no worker/Celery/Prometheus fiction; Redis's real job is now T3's rate limiting. reference-docs: commit the v2 Work Order if David supplied it, else a README noting Parts A-F live in DECISIONS + code; record the spec's 108-vs-implemented-106 subcategory discrepancy. Depends on T2/T3/T5 so it documents what actually shipped.
